folio.label: 168v
folio.l: his peeple

¢ Now sire as to the thridde poynt , where as ©ourö
olde & wyse cou~seilloås seiden , æ ©ow ne ougúte nougút sodeinly
ne hastily ¸ceden in this nede ,

but æ ©ow ougúte påueyen & appa-
reilen ©ow in this cas , w gret diligence , & gret deliberacou~ ,

tre-
wely I trowe , æ they seiden rigút wysely , & rigút sotú

¢ For
Tullius seitú , In euäy nede , er æè bigynne it , apparaile the wiæ
gret diligence

¢ Thanne seye I , æ in vengeance taky~gö , in werre
in bataille , and in warnestoryngö ,

er æè bigynne , I rede æ æè appa-
raile the æä to , and do it , w gret deliberaciou~

¢ For Tullius seitú ;
æ longe apparailyngö biforn~ the bataille , maketú short victorie ,

And Cassidorus seitú ; the garnesou~ is strengere , whan it is lon-
ge tyme a vysed

¢ But now lat vs speken , of æe cou~seil æ was
a corded by ©oure neyghebores , swicú as don ©ow reuäence , w outen
loue ,
©oure olde enemys reconsiled , ©oure flatereres ,

æ cou~seileden
©ow certeyne thynges péuely , & openly cou~seiled ©ow the contrie ;

The ©onge folk also , æ cou~seileden ©ow , to venge ©ow & make werre
a noon ,

And certes sire as I haue seid biforn~ , ©e han gretly
erred , to han cleped swicú manere folk , to ©oure cou~seil ,

whicú
cou~seilloås , ben y otiose dot onth y/ODR/ now reproued by the resons aforseid

Certes the trouthe of this matere
or of this cou~seil , nedetú nat diligently enquere ,

for it is wel wist
whicú they ben , æ han don to ©ow this trespas & vyleynye ,

& how manye trespassours , & in what manere they han don to
©ow , al this wrong , & al this vileynye ,

And after this , tha~ne
shuln ©e examyne , the secund co~dico~n , whicú æ the same
Tullius addetú in this matere ,

For Tullius put a thyngö ,
whicú æ he clepetú , consentynge , This is to seyn ;

who ben
they & whicú ben they , & how manye , æ co~sentyn to thy cou~seil
in thy wilfulnesse , to do hastif vengeau~ce ,

And lat vs considre
also , who ben they , & how manye ben they , & whicú ben they , æ
co~senteden to ©oure aduäsaries

¢ And certes as to the firste poy~t
it is wel knowen , whicú folk ben they , æ co~senteden to ©oure
wilfulnesse ,

For trewely alle tho , æ cou~seileden ©ow , to make~ so-
deyn werre , ne ben nat ©oure frendes

¢ lat vs now co~sidre
whicú ben they , æ ©e holde so gretly ©oure frendes , as to ©oure
¹sone ,

for al be it so , æ ©e be mygúty and riche , certes ©e ne ben
but allone ,
For certes ©e ne han no child , but a dougúter ,

ne ©e
ne han bretheryn , ne Cosyns germayns , ne noon other ny kyn-
rede ,

wherfore æ ©oure enemys , for drede , shulde stynte to ple-
de w ©ow , or destroye ©oure ¹sone

¢ ©e knowen also , æ ©oure
richesses moten ben despended in diuäse ¹ties ,

& whan æ euäy
wigút , hatú his part , they ne wolden taken , but litel reward
to venge thy detú

¢ But thyne enemys ben iijõ , & they han
manye children
